title: Bakewell pudding
subtitle: Flaky pastry case filled with raspberry jam and an almond egg custard
excerpt: The original Bakewell pudding: a crisp shortcrust case spread with raspberry jam and filled with a rich almond and egg custard made from ground almonds, butter, sugar, and eggs. Older and simpler than the better-known tart. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: The Bakewell pudding predates the Bakewell tart and is documented in Derbyshire cookery from the early nineteenth century. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: a pudding made in a pastry case spread with jam and filled with a mixture of eggs, butter, sugar, and ground almonds is documented under regional pastry preparations. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: the Bakewell pudding is recorded as a pastry-lined dish spread with jam and filled with almond cream, noting its origins in the town of Bakewell in Derbyshire.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Can be made a day ahead. Reheat gently in a low oven for 10 minutes or serve at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; blind bake 15 minutes, then fill and bake 22–25 minutes until the almond custard is set with a slight wobble in the centre

content.text: The filling is an almond egg custard, not a sponge. It uses no flour: the ground almonds and eggs set the mixture to a soft, almost fudgy texture as it cools. The jam layer goes in first, before the custard; it does not need to be thick. The pastry should be short and crisp rather than bread-like, so handle it briefly and keep everything cold.

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (pastry portion) into the {{plain-flour}} and {{icing-sugar}} until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add the egg yolk and 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water; press together until the dough just coheres. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 175°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and line the tart tin. Trim and prick the base. Line with paper and baking beans and blind bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper.

Spread the {{jam}} in a thin, even layer over the warm pastry base. Beat the softened {{unsalted-butter}} (filling portion) and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale and fluffy. Beat in the {{eggs}} (filling portion) one at a time. Fold in the {{almond-flour}} until combined. Spoon the almond mixture over the jam, smooth the top, and bake for 22 to 25 minutes until the filling is set with just a slight tremor in the centre when you move the tin. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature with cream.
